title: Cisco ISE pxGrid Cloud Connectivity and TLS Health
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` from Splunkbase 1915. pxGrid Cloud (introduced ISE 3.1) emits under the *pxGrid Cloud* category — message codes `82200` (cloud connection established), `82201` (cloud connection lost), `82202` (cloud TLS handshake failed), `82203` (cloud-relay rate-limit), `82210` (cloud subscriber subscribed via cloud relay). The pxGrid Cloud feature is required to integrate ISE with cloud-hosted SOC platforms (Cisco SecureX Cloud, Cisco XDR Cloud, Splunk Cloud SOAR via the cloud connector). Fields: `CloudRelay`, `CloudPeer`, `TLSCipher`, `TLSVersion`. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/pxgrid/cloud/status` polled every 5 min for the live cloud-tunnel health, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:pxgrid_cloud_status`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (82200, 82201, 82202, 82203))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:pxgrid_cloud_status")
| stats latest(_time) as last_event, latest(connection_state) as state, latest(TLSVersion) as tls_version, latest(TLSCipher) as cipher, count(eval(MessageCode=="82201")) as conn_drops, count(eval(MessageCode=="82202")) as tls_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="82203")) as rate_limits by CloudPeer
| where conn_drops > 1 OR tls_failures > 0 OR rate_limits > 0 OR state=="DISCONNECTED" OR tls_version IN ("TLSv1.0", "TLSv1.1")
| sort - tls_failures - conn_drops
description: Detects pxGrid Cloud connection drops, TLS handshake failures, weak TLS versions in use, and cloud-relay rate-limit hits. The pxGrid Cloud relay is the conduit between an on-prem ISE deployment and cloud-hosted SOC platforms (Cisco XDR, Cisco SecureX Cloud).
value: When pxGrid Cloud is the path to your cloud-hosted SOC, a broken cloud relay means cloud detection rules run on stale identity context. Catching the disconnect within 10 minutes preserves the data integrity of cloud-side correlation. TLS-version drift to TLSv1.0/v1.1 evidences a cipher-policy regression that fails PCI DSS 4.0 and FIPS 140-3.
implementation: Confirm the *pxGrid Cloud* logging category is enabled at INFO. Deploy the OpenAPI poller for `/api/v1/pxgrid/cloud/status` every 5 min. Schedule the search every 10 min, search window `-30m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `CloudPeer` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single-value: cloud-tunnel state (CONNECTED/DISCONNECTED). (2) Timechart: connection events last 24h. (3) TLS version compliance pie: count of sessions by TLSVersion last 30d. (4) Top-N table: peers with most connection drops or TLS failures last 7d.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — pxGrid Cloud
Cisco XDR — Integration with ISE via pxGrid Cloud
Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (Splunkbase 1915)

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Internet maintenance windows** — single connection-drop events that self-clear within 5 min are not paging-worthy. (b) **Cisco-side cloud-relay maintenance** — Cisco occasionally maintenances the cloud relay, briefly dropping connections fleet-wide. Cisco posts these on the trust-center status page; cross-reference and downgrade to notification. (c) **TLS-version warnings on TLSv1.2** — TLSv1.2 is acceptable for most regulatory regimes; the alert flags only TLSv1.0/v1.1. Do not page on TLSv1.2 unless your policy requires TLSv1.3-only.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 4.2 requires strong cryptography on transmission. pxGrid Cloud TLS-version monitoring evidences ongoing maintenance of the cryptographic boundary for ISE-to-cloud-SOC traffic.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence operational maintenance of TLS strength on pxGrid Cloud transmissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.35 alert log + monthly TLS-version-distribution report.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SC-8(1) (Cryptographic Protection) requires cryptographic mechanisms to protect transmission. TLS monitoring evidences operational maintenance.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence operational cryptographic protection of pxGrid Cloud transmissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.35 alert log + TLS report retained 1y.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.20 (Networks security) requires controls on networks to protect information in systems and applications. Cloud-relay TLS monitoring is one such control.
compliance.controlObjective: Maintain network-security controls on the cloud-relay path.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab ISE, deliberately misconfigure the TLS cipher list to include only deprecated ciphers and force a reconnect. ISE emits 82202 (TLS handshake failed). UC-17.1.35 fires within 10 min showing the peer with `tls_failures > 0`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore the TLS cipher list to defaults. The next reconnect succeeds. The next 30-min window shows zero new TLS failures and the alert clears.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE 3.1+ with pxGrid Cloud configured (Administration > pxGrid Services > Cloud Connection).
• Trusted root chain for the pxGrid Cloud relay imported into ISE Trusted Certificates.
• `pxgrid_cloud_peers.csv` lookup with `peer_name`, `purpose`, `is_critical`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *pxGrid Cloud* logging category at INFO. Confirm:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (82200, 82201, 82202) earliest=-7d
| stats count by MessageCode, CloudPeer
```
Deploy the OpenAPI poller every 5 min on `/api/v1/pxgrid/cloud/status`.

Step 2 — Create the search
```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (82200, 82201, 82202, 82203))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:pxgrid_cloud_status")
| stats latest(_time) as last_event, latest(connection_state) as state, latest(TLSVersion) as tls_version, latest(TLSCipher) as cipher, count(eval(MessageCode=="82201")) as conn_drops, count(eval(MessageCode=="82202")) as tls_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="82203")) as rate_limits by CloudPeer
| where conn_drops > 1 OR tls_failures > 0 OR rate_limits > 0 OR state=="DISCONNECTED" OR tls_version IN ("TLSv1.0", "TLSv1.1")
| sort - tls_failures - conn_drops
```

Schedule: every 10 min, range `-30m@m to @m`, throttle `CloudPeer` for 30 min.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Provoke a test as above.
(b) Verify state transitions in syslog align with the OpenAPI snapshot.
(c) Inspect TLS version distribution monthly.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — pxGrid Cloud Health":
• Connection state tile.
• 24h timechart of connection events.
• TLS version distribution pie.
• Top-N table of peers with failures.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium-urgency on TLS failure or sustained DISCONNECTED. Auto-create SOAR ticket on weak TLS version detection.

Runbook:
1. Identify peer and event.
2. Verify ISE node has internet reachability (proxy/firewall).
3. Validate the trusted root chain for pxGrid Cloud is current.
4. If TLS version is weak, identify whether the issue is on ISE side (cipher policy regression) or cloud-relay side (server config).
5. Engage Cisco TAC for cloud-relay-side issues.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Persistent DISCONNECTED state** — Root chain or proxy auth. SSH to ISE: `show crypto ca certificates` and verify the pxGrid Cloud trust anchor is present and not expired. If using an outbound proxy, validate the proxy auth (Administration > System > Settings > Proxy).

• **TLSv1.0 in use** — Cipher-policy regression. Check the `tls_version` configuration in pxGrid Cloud settings; force TLSv1.2+ minimum.

• **Connection drops every hour exactly** — DHCP renewal on ISE outbound NAT IP, or stateful firewall idle timeout. Adjust firewall idle timeout or static-NAT the ISE outbound IP.

• **TA misses 822xx codes** — TA version <5.0 didn't include pxGrid Cloud parsing. Upgrade Splunkbase 1915 to 5.0+.
grandmaExplanation: If a cloud security tool listens to ISE through a long-distance call, we make sure the call stays connected and uses a strong, modern phone line — and ring the bell if the call drops, fails to connect, or downgrades to an old protocol.
